parameterdescription.para: LO frequency in cycles/sample at the input rate.
Output rate / input rate. Must be > 0.

simplesect.para: Non-NULL on success, NULL on OOM or invalid args.

detaileddescription.para: Free all resources. NULL is a no-op.
Zero LO phase and resampler history.
Return the current LO normalised frequency.

briefdescription.para: Retune the LO without resetting phase or resampler history.

detaileddescription.para: Return the configured output/input rate ratio.

briefdescription.para: Return the maximum output samples for one execute call.

detaileddescription.para: Returns 0, signalling the Python extension to fall back to allocating n_in samples — always sufficient for a decimating DDC.

memberdef.argsstring: (ddc_state_t *s, const float _Complex *in, size_t n_in, float _Complex *out, size_t max_out)
memberdef.name: ddc_execute

parameterdescription.para: Fine NCO frequency at the intermediate rate (fs_in/2).
Total output/input rate. Must be in (0, 0.5).

simplesect.para: Non-NULL on success, NULL on OOM or invalid args.

detaileddescription.para: Free all resources. NULL is a no-op.
Zero halfband, LO phase, and resampler history.
Return the current fine NCO normalised frequency (at intermediate rate).
Retune the fine NCO without resetting state.
Return the total configured rate (fs_out / fs_in).

memberdef.argsstring: (ddcr_state_t *s, const float *in, size_t n_in, float _Complex *out, size_t max_out)
memberdef.name: ddcr_execute

briefdescription.para: Process a block of real float32 samples.

parameterdescription.para: Real input samples, float32, length n_in.

detaileddescription.para: Ddc: lo_steps → element-wise multiply → RateConverter_execute DdcR: hbdecim_r2c_execute → lo_steps → element-wise multiply → RateConverter_execute
Both implementations allocate temporary buffers per execute() call. For typical SDR block sizes (1 k–64 k samples) the Python GIL and NumPy overhead dominate the malloc cost.
RateConverter selects the cheapest cascade (CIC + optional HB + polyphase) at create time, matching the rate automatically.
